mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the UNRATE threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of consumer credit (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between joint contraction and non-contraction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 315, an increase in the federal funds rate fails to stabilize equity valuations because real rates remain negative, resulting in continued negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns become positive when FEDFUNDS rises during CPI > 315 periods, or the predictive power of the regime-filtered signal vanishes in a 2010-2020 sample.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects robust consumer confidence and credit availability, which supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5d forward returns in rising HOUST months are not statistically distinct from flat or declining months.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.5% annualized while the unemployment rate remains above 4.0%, the economy enters a confirmed slowdown that triggers a risk-off repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production (INDPRO) rises while consumer credit (RSAFS) falls indicates a supply-driven inventory buildup that will force corporate margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The cross-product term of INDPRO and RSAFS changes fails to predict returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window, or forward returns are indistinguishable from the baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid employment growth where PAYEMS rises above 150,000 month-over-month, an acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) amplifies capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to earnings revision momentum.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when PAYEMS and INDPRO both accelerate, or the signal's hit rate drops below 50% in a 2008-2023 sample.
